summary: Lactotransferrin has no documented role as a tumor suppressor according to current medical literature; there is insufficient evidence supporting this function despite some general roles for similar iron-binding proteins in immune regulation. This lack of specific data leads to classifying lactotransferrin as non-tumor suppressing within known clinical contexts.
